title: Founding Operator (Jill Delivery)
key_evidence: Role sits on the Jill Delivery team = post-sale account management, customer success, expansion, escalations, owning a customer portfolio. Ryan has community/investor/PR-facing growth but NO enterprise account-management or B2B customer-success portfolio ownership — core requirements gap.

Wants prior customer-portfolio ownership + hiring involvement + light scripting/automation. Ryan has hiring-support (Founder's Associate) and Claude Code automation — partial fit; portfolio-ownership is the weak spot.

'Convert manual processes into repeatable workflows' + 'structured customer insights to product' maps to Ryan's AI-agent / repeatable-research-workflow framing and first-employee operator background — genuine spike overlap.

Funding: $20M seed led by Creandum (tier-1), >£1m ARR, 10 months old, ~15 people (10 ex-founders). Well-funded + buzzy AI-recruiting + high comp => high applicant volume and an exceptionally high ex-founder peer bar.
Salary £100-180k + flexible equity, MacBook/Bupa/perks — far above £50k floor (positive want), but the comp band signals a senior/high-bar hire that may stretch Ryan's mid-level operator profile.
Small founder-heavy team, Ashby direct apply, works directly with Jill Delivery Lead — founder/lead-read screening, not recruiter keyword filter.
biggest_lever: Position the first-employee 1->30 scaling + 'turn ambiguous founder priorities into workstreams' + Claude Code workflow automation as exactly the high-agency process-builder this seat needs.
biggest_risk: Requirement centre of gravity is enterprise customer-success / account-portfolio ownership, which Ryan lacks; competing against a 10-ex-founder bar for a £100-180k seat where peers likely have direct CS/AM track records.
